# snapgrab

> URL to screenshot with metadata. Python MCP server included. Claude Vision optimized.

## Purpose

Captures screenshots of web pages using Playwright and extracts page metadata (title, Open Graph tags, favicon). Automatically optimizes output images for Claude Vision by resizing to 1568px max dimension, reducing token cost while preserving visual fidelity.

## Capabilities

- **Screenshot capture**: Full-page or viewport-only captures via Playwright in PNG, JPEG, or PDF format
- **Viewport presets**: desktop (1920x1080), tablet (768x1024), mobile (375x812), or custom WxH
- **Claude Vision optimization**: Auto-resize to 1568px max with vision token estimation
- **Metadata extraction**: Title, description, Open Graph tags, favicon URL, HTTP status
- **Element capture**: Target specific elements via CSS selector
- **Comparison mode**: Capture same URL in multiple viewports for responsive design review
- **Dark mode**: Force dark color scheme for captures

## Installation

```
pip install snapgrab
pip install snapgrab[mcp]    # + MCP server
pip install snapgrab[all]    # everything
playwright install chromium  # required browser
```

## Quick Use

### CLI

```bash
snapgrab url https://example.com
snapgrab url https://example.com --viewport mobile --full-page --format jpeg
snapgrab url https://example.com --selector ".main-content" --dark-mode
snapgrab url https://example.com --output ./screenshots --json-output
snapgrab meta https://example.com
```

### Python

```python
from snapgrab import capture

result = await capture("https://example.com", viewport="desktop", full_page=True)
print(result.path, result.vision_tokens, result.metadata.title)
```

### MCP

Server: `snapgrab` (command: `snapgrab-mcp`)
Tools: `capture_screenshot`, `capture_comparison`, `extract_page_metadata`

## Links

- Docs: https://github.com/QuartzUnit/snapgrab
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/snapgrab/
- Source: https://github.com/QuartzUnit/snapgrab
